task_completion.description: Import the survey data file into TopoCal
task_completion.visual_evidence: The TopoCal application window is open and points from 'foothills_local_grid.txt' are loaded and visible in the drawing area or point list.

task_completion.description: Set the rotation angle and center point
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rotation dialog, command line, or tool settings panel in TopoCal shows an angle of 45 degrees (counter-clockwise) and a base/center point of X=5000, Y=5000.

task_completion.description: Apply the rotation to the points
task_completion.visual_evidence: The points in the TopoCal drawing area are visibly rotated, or a success message/log indicates the rotation was successfully applied to the points.
A 'Save As' dialog saving the project as 'rotated_project.top' in the Documents folder, or the TopoCal title bar showing 'rotated_project.top'.

task_completion.description: Export the modified points to a CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog saving the points as 'rotated_points.csv' in the Documents folder, or the file visible in the Windows File Explorer.

integrity.description: The agent used the TopoCal graphical interface to perform the rotation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with TopoCal's menus, dialogs, or built-in command line, rather than using external Python scripts or terminal commands to process the data.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually create or edit the output files using a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no instances of Notepad or other text editors being used to manually type out the rotated coordinates or fake the .top/.csv files.

integrity.description: The output files were generated by TopoCal
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'rotated_project.top' and 'rotated_points.csv' files should be created via TopoCal's save and export functionalities, not by copying, renaming, or echoing text in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires rotating a 7-point grid by exactly 45 degrees counter-clockwise around Point 1 (X: 5000.0, Y: 5000.0). The Z elevations (e.g., 1850.0 for Point 1) must remain unchanged. The expected outputs are 'rotated_project.top' and 'rotated_points.csv' in the C:\Users\Docker\Documents\ folder.
